basics.name: Aditya Singh
basics.label: UCSB Computer Science student building AI/LLM systems and full-stack products

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

work.name: Ryft AI
highlights: Architected a scalable Python and FastAPI backend spanning 40 REST endpoints for a 3-tier multi-agent orchestrator, driving platform growth to 30k ARR and securing enterprise deployments with YC-backed clients like Corgi AI via CRM integrations

Designed an autonomous agentic loop using GPT-4o equipped with 12 discrete tools for complex data transformation, utilizing strict 22-field JSON constraint prompting to achieve 95% extraction accuracy across unstructured PDF contracts

Engineered a multi-tier context management system using pgvector RAG and cross-session memory compaction, pairing domain-aware chunking with Bedrock prompt caching to reduce per-session LLM costs by 60–70%, ensuring accuracy

Developed a 3-layer observability and evaluation framework implementing an AI validation agent that cross-checks generated logic against source contracts, eliminating 350–400 hours of manual QA work per quarter ensuring auditability

work.name: Techno G.O.A.Ts LLC
highlights: Co-founded a 501(c)(3) robotics nonprofit—3D-modeled 30+ competition robots in Fusion 360, trained machine learning and ran weekly build sprints synthesizing build team progress for efficient hardware-code integration

Engineered a custom computer vision pipeline using TensorFlow and OpenCV to train a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), reducing autonomous task latency by 40%+ during competitive matches with 96% object detection accuracy
Mentored neurodivergent learners via a 4-year Serendipity STEM partnership, and secured a 5k Apple grant

projects.name: Ziri
projects.description: Distributed voice OS with LangGraph supervisor routing across specialized ReAct subagents
highlights: Architected a distributed Python/FastAPI voice OS with a LangGraph supervisor routing complex intents across 3 specialized ReAct subagents (25+ tools), while handling 80% of commands via a deterministic zero-LLM fast path

Engineered a semantic memory system using 1536-dim Amazon Titan embeddings and Supabase pgvector with HNSW indexing. Queried history via SQL similarity search to inject user preferences into Claude Sonnet 4.6 context windows

Developed an evaluation and observability framework using Langfuse to trace generation spans and score tool-execution correctness; optimized the ElevenLabs streaming pipeline to achieve sub-50ms audio latency for cached interactions and sub-900ms TTFB for complex multi-step voice queries

projects.name: Autonomous Soft Robot System for Safer Brain Tumor Therapy
projects.description: ML-guided controller for flexible surgical robots with MRI-based collision-aware planning
highlights: Trained a Bayesian Neural Network on 100k joint configs via Matlab's Neural Net Toolbox to predict a flexible surgical robot's 3D tip position from joint inputs: Achieved sub-0.1% max tip error on unseen targets
Developed a MATLAB algorithm to convert multi-million-voxel MRI data into interactive 3D brain safety maps enabling high-fidelity collision-aware planning with tumor highlighting
Engineered a C++ control stack for a 6-DOF robot arm through an Arduino, streaming PC-to-Arduino communication and synchronizing multi-actuator motion
Won 1st place among 230+ participants at regional science and engineering fair (ACSEF)

publications.name: Kinematic Controller of a Soft Continuum Robot Using Learned Forward Models
highlights: Formalized a learned forward-kinematics algorithm for soft continuum robots over 6 months of research, addressing redundancy and non-invertibility
Presented at the IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference
